independent_tests.test_output_summary: Independent test verification skipped (tests not required for testing tasks)

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py` walks `src/jarvis/` recursively and asserts the following strings DO NOT appear in any `.py`, `.yaml`, or `.txt` file: `deep_reasoner`, `adversarial_critic`, `long_research`, `quick_local` (scenario: *The jarvis-reasoner description does not mention the retired four-subagent roster*).
criteria_verification.evidence: tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py defines TestAC001SourceTreeFreeOfRetiredRosterNames which uses Path.rglob over src/jarvis (skipping __pycache__), filters by the .py / .yaml / .yml / .txt extensions, and parametrises an `in`-substring assertion across every retired roster name. To make the test green, the prior occurrences in src/jarvis/prompts/supervisor_prompt.py and src/jarvis/agents/subagent_registry.py docstrings/comments were rewritten to point at the regression test rather than enumerating the names inline.
criteria_verification.notes: tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py defines TestAC001SourceTreeFreeOfRetiredRosterNames which uses Path.rglob over src/jarvis (skipping __pycache__), filters by the .py / .yaml / .yml / .txt extensions, and parametrises an `in`-substring assertion across every retired roster name. To make the test green, the prior occurrences in src/jarvis/prompts/supervisor_prompt.py and src/jarvis/agents/subagent_registry.py docstrings/comments were rewritten to point at the regression test rather than enumerating the names inline.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Same test — asserts the rendered `SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` contains none of the four retired names.
criteria_verification.evidence: TestAC002SupervisorPromptFreeOfRetiredNames in tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py renders SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT via str.format with placeholder values for {date}/{available_capabilities}/{domain_prompt}, then parametrises a substring assertion over the four retired roster names. The supervisor_prompt.py docstring update keeps the rendered prompt free of those tokens.
criteria_verification.notes: TestAC002SupervisorPromptFreeOfRetiredNames in tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py renders SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT via str.format with placeholder values for {date}/{available_capabilities}/{domain_prompt}, then parametrises a substring assertion over the four retired roster names. The supervisor_prompt.py docstring update keeps the rendered prompt free of those tokens.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Same test — asserts `SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` contains none of the retired JA6 cloud-fallback phrases (e.g. no mention of "vllm fallback", "gemini-flash-latest", "cloud cheap-tier").
criteria_verification.evidence: TestAC003SupervisorPromptFreeOfJA6CloudFallback in tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py lower-cases the rendered SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT and asserts that none of the JA6 cloud-fallback phrases (`vllm fallback`, `gemini-flash-latest`, `cloud cheap-tier`) appear. The current prompt does not contain any of these tokens, so the test passes without further source edits.
criteria_verification.notes: TestAC003SupervisorPromptFreeOfJA6CloudFallback in tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py lower-cases the rendered SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT and asserts that none of the JA6 cloud-fallback phrases (`vllm fallback`, `gemini-flash-latest`, `cloud cheap-tier`) appear. The current prompt does not contain any of these tokens, so the test passes without further source edits.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py` asserts the `AsyncSubAgent` description from `build_async_subagents(test_config)[0].description` contains ALL required signal substrings: `gpt-oss-120b`, `on the premises`, `sub-second`, `two to four minutes`, `critic`, `researcher`, `planner`.
criteria_verification.evidence: tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py defines TestAC004RequiredRoutingSignals which calls build_async_subagents(test_config)[0]['description'] and parametrises a substring `in` assertion over every required signal, plus a single all-at-once test that reports any missing signals together.
criteria_verification.notes: tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py defines TestAC004RequiredRoutingSignals which calls build_async_subagents(test_config)[0]['description'] and parametrises a substring `in` assertion over every required signal, plus a single all-at-once test that reports any missing signals together.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Same file — asserts the description does NOT promise cloud-tier reasoning (no `cloud`, `GPT`, `Claude`, `Gemini`, `OpenAI`, `Anthropic` tokens — the model name `gpt-oss-120b` is local-fleet, not cloud).
criteria_verification.evidence: TestAC005NoCloudTierPromise in tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py parametrises across the six forbidden cloud-tier tokens and asserts that the hyphen-aware word-boundary regex returns no match against the description string. Because the regex treats hyphens as token-internal, `gpt-oss-120b` does not produce a `GPT` match.
criteria_verification.notes: TestAC005NoCloudTierPromise in tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py parametrises across the six forbidden cloud-tier tokens and asserts that the hyphen-aware word-boundary regex returns no match against the description string. Because the regex treats hyphens as token-internal, `gpt-oss-120b` does not produce a `GPT` match.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Note: the `gpt-oss-120b` substring contains `gpt` — the assertion uses word-boundary regex so it does not trigger on this model-name token alone.
criteria_verification.evidence: TestAC006HyphenAwareWordBoundary in tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py exercises the regex shape `(?<![A-Za-z0-9-])TOKEN(?![A-Za-z0-9-])` (case-insensitive) with: a positive sample where `gpt-oss-120b` MUST NOT match GPT; explicit positive samples where standalone `GPT`/`gpt` MUST match; and a Gemini/Claude cross-check showing hyphenated identifiers (`gemini-3.1-pro`, `claude-3.5`) are tolerated while standalone tokens are rejected.
criteria_verification.notes: TestAC006HyphenAwareWordBoundary in tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py exercises the regex shape `(?<![A-Za-z0-9-])TOKEN(?![A-Za-z0-9-])` (case-insensitive) with: a positive sample where `gpt-oss-120b` MUST NOT match GPT; explicit positive samples where standalone `GPT`/`gpt` MUST match; and a Gemini/Claude cross-check showing hyphenated identifiers (`gemini-3.1-pro`, `claude-3.5`) are tolerated while standalone tokens are rejected.

criteria_verification.evidence: `uv run pytest tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py -v` executed locally and reported 29 passed in 0.36s (no failures, no errors). The full suite of 1442 tests also passes.
criteria_verification.notes: `uv run pytest tests/test_no_retired_roster_strings.py tests/test_jarvis_reasoner_description.py -v` executed locally and reported 29 passed in 0.36s (no failures, no errors). The full suite of 1442 tests also passes.
